what_it_does: A posted noticeboard note by Tim in the dragnet-development channel that comments on item-043cb37b, acknowledging a related design idea about addressable gaps between blocks and clarifying it is not quite what he meant by 'the space' while expressing interest in exploring it.
observations: YAML frontmatter records id item-e1644d19, address board://item-e1644d19, type note, source claude_code, state posted, title 'Tim · point', author_session tim, channel dragnet-development.
A links entry of kind commented_on points to board://item-043cb37b.
Created and updated timestamps are identical, indicating an initial post with no edits beyond filing.

Body begins with a [point] marker and references 'Zones' (gaps between blocks) as addressable nodes, comparing them to Google Docs insertion-point comments and code-review whole-file / between-lines comments.
The note proposes modeling a zone as an anchor between two sibling node-ids, i.e., an edge rather than a node, to survive re-parse like a block.
Tim then clarifies that this is not exactly what he meant about 'the space,' but calls it interesting and says he wants to hear more.
standouts: The design proposal to treat gaps between blocks as addressable zones anchored by edges between sibling node-ids.
Tim's explicit clarification that the zone idea is not what he originally meant by 'the space'.
The genuine interest signal: 'Interesting ideas, ones that I actually do want to hear more about.'

notes: Tim posts a short design reaction: the 'addressable zones between blocks' idea is not what he meant by 'the space,' but it is interesting and he wants to hear more.
